reasoning: Initial observation provides a baseline to start exploring the relationship between m and other variables.
Increasing rho did not change m, suggesting m is not directly dependent on rho. Next, I will explore the effect of changing V.
Increasing V doubled m, suggesting m might be proportional to V squared.
Doubling V doubled m, indicating a quadratic relationship. Decreasing V back to its original value confirms the relationship. Now, I will check the effect of changing k.
Increasing k from 3 to 4 while keeping V and rho constant increased m from 90 to 120, suggesting a direct proportionality between m and k.
